title: Agent run status snapshot
description: Status drop for one agent run (one cmux/tmux session driving a command over one issue). Written atomically (mktemp + mv) by emit-status.sh at each phase boundary; read by dashboard.py. Runtime-only: these live in ${AGENT_DASHBOARD_STATE_DIR:-$HOME/.claude/agent-dashboard/state}/, never in a repo.

session.description: Unique id for this run (cmux pane id, tmux session, or issue+role). Also the snapshot filename stem.

state.description: Coarse lifecycle state. Fine-grained progress lives on the issue/PR, not here. 'waiting' = paused at a routine human gate (expected flow); 'escalated' = urgent, off-happy-path (failure, hard stop) - the one that flags 'needs you NOW'. Red is earned.

iso_timestamp.description: RFC3339 UTC timestamp of this status write.

epoch.description: Unix seconds at write time. Used for age math without re-parsing iso_timestamp.

ticket.description: The issue id this run owns, when issue-bound (free-form: PROJ-123, #123, a URL, etc.).

pr_number.description: GitHub PR number once a PR is open (string to tolerate empty/absent).

worktree_path.description: Absolute path to the run's git worktree on disk.

model.description: Which model drives this run (e.g. opus, sonnet, haiku, a full id, or a non-Claude agent such as grok or codex). Set via --model, else auto-detected from $AGENT_DASHBOARD_MODEL or cmux's launch argv — auto-detect only understands Claude/cmux launches, so non-Claude agents must pass --model explicitly.

cmux_surface.description: The emitting session's cmux surface UUID ($CMUX_SURFACE_ID), captured best-effort by emit-status.sh. dashboard.py matches it against `cmux tree` for pane liveness. Absent outside cmux.

note.description: One-line human-readable status the operator sees in the dashboard.
